children.name: node2-1-1-1-2-1-1-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Extended partial STRING_GNN fine-tuning to include mps.4, mps.5 as trainable (backbone_lr2=2e-6) alongside mps.6/7/post_mp (backbone_lr=1e-5); mps.0-3 remain frozen as precomputed buffer
- Replaced focal loss + label smoothing with SGDR scheduler (T_0=20, T_mult=2)
- Replaced post-hoc 2-model SWA with quality-filtered top-8 SWA (val_f1 ≥ 0.498 threshold)
- Reduced weight decay from 4e-3 to 3e-3

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: **0.5113** (parent: 0.5118, delta: **-0.0005**; sibling: 0.5124, delta: **-0.0011**)
- Best single checkpoint: val_f1=**0.5112** at epoch 86 (+0.0014 vs parent's 0.5098; +0.0028 vs sibling's 0.5084)
- Training duration: 237 epochs, 4 SGDR cycles
- SWA contribution: **+0.0001** (vs sibling's +0.0040 from uniform 23-checkpoint averaging)
- Cycle 1 peak val F1: 0.4975 (vs sibling's 0.5011, delta: -0.0036)
- Cycle 3 val plateau: mean=0.5021, std=0.0046 (epochs 60-100)
- Quality-filtered SWA selection range: val_f1 0.5009–0.5063 (8 near-identical checkpoints)
- Train loss trajectory: 0.4287→0.1255 (monotonically decreasing)

**Key Issues**
- SWA diversity collapse: quality-filtered top-8 selection selected only near-identical plateau configurations, yielding negligible SWA gain (+0.0001) compared to sibling's broad uniform averaging (+0.0040)
- SGDR T_0=20 too short for adequate cycle 1 convergence (peak 0.4975 vs sibling's 0.5011)
- backbone_lr2=2e-6 may be too conservative for meaningful mps.4, mps.5 adaptation
- Despite achieving highest single-checkpoint val_f1 in lineage (0.5112), test F1 underperformed due to SWA ineffectiveness

children.name: node2-1-1-1-2-1-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Added SGDR warm-restart schedule (T_0=40 epochs, T_mult=2) to escape local optima
- Implemented comprehensive periodic SWA over 10 checkpoints (every 20 epochs + best)
- Increased dropout from 0.4 to 0.45 and weight decay from 2e-3 to 3e-3

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5054 (−0.0048 vs parent 0.5102, −0.0064 vs sibling 0.5118)
- Best validation F1: 0.5106 at epoch 61 (+0.0005 vs parent 0.5101)
- SGDR cycle 1 peak: val_f1=0.5006 at epoch 34
- SGDR cycle 2 peak: val_f1=0.5106 at epoch 61 (warm restart at epoch ~44)
- SGDR cycle 3 peak: val_f1=0.5061 at epoch 157 (diminishing returns)
- SWA dilution effect: single best (0.5106) → SWA average (0.5054) = −0.0052 regression

**Key Issues**
- SWA quality dilution: averaging 10 checkpoints uniformly included low-quality checkpoints (epoch 59: val_f1=0.4961, epoch 179: val_f1=0.4934), dragging ensemble down by 0.0052 F1
- SGDR warm restart at epoch ~44 successfully improved cycle peak (+0.010 F1), but cycle 3 failed to improve on cycle 2's peak
- Comprehensive SWA strategy failed to deliver expected gains due to lack of selective filtering

children.name: node2-1-1-1-2-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Increased dropout from 0.3 to 0.45 in the 6-layer bilinear residual MLP head
- Increased label smoothing epsilon from 0.05 to 0.10
- Increased weight decay from 2e-3 (implied from parent context) to 4e-3
- Replaced cosine LR schedule with SGDR micro-cycles (T_0=20 epochs=440 steps with 2 GPUs, T_mult=2.0)
- Added quality-filtered exponential SWA (top-15 checkpoints, threshold=0.497, temperature=3.0)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5094 (+0.0006 vs parent 0.5088, -0.0008 vs sibling 0.5102)
- Best validation F1: 0.5082 at epoch 86 (parent: 0.5087 at epoch 52)
- SWA gain: +0.0012 over single best checkpoint
- Training epochs: 287 with early stopping at patience=200 from epoch 86 (parent: 152 epochs)
- Training dynamics: rapid ascent (val_f1 to 0.497 by epoch 24), oscillatory plateau (cycle 2 peak=0.5081 at epoch 40, cycle 3 peak=0.5082 at epoch 86, cycle 4 peak=0.5077 at epoch 179), slow decline (val_f1 to 0.4942 at epoch 286)
- Final train/val loss ratio: 2.076 at epoch 286

**Key Issues**
- SGDR ascending staircase failed after cycle 2: cycles 3 and 4 showed minimal gains (+0.0001 and -0.0005 vs cycle 2 peak), not the expected +0.006 cycle gains seen in tree-best lineage
- Over-regularization bottleneck: combined dropout=0.45 + weight_decay=4e-3 appears too aggressive, preventing escape from cycle 2 performance basin (~0.508)
- SWA pool had nearly uniform exponential weights (range 0.0663-0.0675 across 15 checkpoints, epochs 35-140, val_f1 0.5020-0.5081), limiting ensemble diversity and yielding only +0.0012 gain
- Validation set noise (157 samples) contributed to wide Phase 2 oscillations

children.name: node2-1-1-1-2-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Partial STRING_GNN fine-tuning (mps.6+7+post_mp at backbone_lr=1e-5, ~198K additional params) vs frozen backbone
- Dropout increased from 0.2 to 0.4 in the 6-layer deep bilinear MLP head
- Pre-initialization strategy: output gene embeddings from STRING_GNN intermediate representations (not just PPI topology)
- Extended cosine schedule: total_steps=2500 (vs implicit shorter schedule) with warmup=150
- SWA checkpoint pool mechanism introduced (top-15 checkpoints collected)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5116 (new tree best) vs parent 0.5012 (+0.0104) vs sibling 0.5088 (+0.0028)
- Best validation F1: 0.5116 at epoch 43 vs parent 0.5010 at epoch 23
- Final val F1 at termination: 0.4954 at epoch 163 (patience=120) vs parent 0.4877 at epoch 103
- Training dynamics: narrower oscillation swing std=0.0052 (vs sibling's wider swings) with peak at epoch 43
- Val/train loss ratio: 2.40x at best epoch, 3.23x at termination (vs parent gap of 0.044 vs 0.287)

**Key Issues**
- Best epoch arrived earlier than expected (epoch 43 vs predicted 60-80), causing extended cosine tail with no performance gain
- SWA checkpoint pool was collected but never applied to test inference, leaving ~0.001-0.003 F1 gain unutilized
- Moderate overfitting: val/train loss ratio increased from 2.40x to 3.23x during training
- Post-peak plateau phase (epochs 56-163) showed no recovery despite extended training

children.name: node2-1-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Replaced AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA + naive gene-position input with STRING_GNN partial fine-tuning framework (trainable: mps.6, mps.7, post_mp ~198K params; frozen: emb+mps.0-5 precomputed as buffer)
- Replaced compact bilinear interaction head with 6-layer deep residual bilinear MLP head (hidden=512, expand=4, dropout=0.25, bilinear_rank=512, ~17.1M total trainable params)
- Replaced single focal loss with class-weighted focal loss (γ=2.0, weights=[2.0, 0.5, 4.0])
- Implemented two-group AdamW optimizer (backbone lr=5e-5, head lr=5e-4, weight_decay=1.5e-3)
- Calibrated cosine schedule to total_steps≈1200 (100-epoch horizon), addressing parent's flat LR throughout training

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.5000 (best val F1=0.5000 at epoch 64)
- Vs parent node2-1-1: +0.0746 F1 improvement (parent: 0.4254)
- Vs sibling node2-1-1-1: +0.0482 F1 improvement
- Vs MCTS tree best (node2-1-3): −0.0047 gap (tree best: 0.5047)
- Training: 125 epochs, three phases — rapid improvement (epochs 0–10, val F1 0.255→0.464), gradual improvement via LR decay (epochs 10–64, val F1 0.464→0.500), plateau (epochs 64–124, val F1 ≈ 0.494, std ≈ 0.001)
- Calibration overfitting: val loss increased 29.4% from minimum (0.1725 at epoch 10 → 0.2232 at epoch 124); train loss 0.0681; val/train ratio 3.28×

**Key Issues**
- Moderate calibration overfitting throughout training (val loss up 29.4%, train loss down to 0.0681, 3.28× ratio)
- Architectural capacity ceiling reached at ~0.500 val F1 with no secondary improvement phase despite calibrated LR schedule and patience=60
- Gap to tree best (−0.0047) constrained by calibration drift rather than optimization convergence

children.name: node2-1-1-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Expanded head: 8-layer residual bilinear MLP (rank=768, ~22.2M params, +30% vs parent's 6-layer rank=512, ~17.1M)
- Label smoothing: added ε=0.05 to class-weighted focal loss
- Lighter regularization: weight_decay reduced 1.5e-3→1e-3, dropout reduced 0.25→0.20
- Adjusted class weights: [2.0, 0.5, 4.0]→[2.0, 0.5, 5.0]

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5004 vs parent 0.5000 (+0.0004)
- Best val F1: 0.5003 at epoch 42 (single-epoch spike)
- Stable val F1 plateau: ~0.491 (epochs 43-102, std≈0.003)
- Gap to tree best (node2-1-3): -0.0043 (0.5004 vs 0.5047)
- Val/train loss ratio improved: 2.82× vs parent 3.28×
- Training duration: 103 epochs vs parent 125 epochs

**Key Issues**
- Marginal F1 improvement (+0.0004) fails to close gap to tree best (-0.0043)
- Persistent ~0.500 F1 ceiling across rank=512 and rank=768 configurations
- Bilinear factorization design appears at effective capacity for 1,416 samples
- Best val F1 (0.5003) was noisy single-epoch spike, not stable peak
- True stable performance ~0.492, below target ≥0.505

children.name: node2-1-1-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- STRING_GNN partial fine-tuning (mps.6+7+post_mp, ~198K trainable backbone params)
- 6-layer residual bilinear head (rank=512, ~18.5M params)
- Muon optimizer on ResidualBlock 2D weights (muon_lr=0.005)
- SGDR warm restarts (T_0=20, T_mult=2, 4 cycles, 231 epochs)
- Post-hoc quality-filtered SWA (top-20, threshold=0.480, temperature=3.0)
- Regularization: head_dropout=0.40, weight_decay=3e-3, label_smoothing=0.07

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Best val_f1=0.5101 at epoch 80 (cycle 3 peak) vs parent ceiling
- Test F1=0.4966 vs sibling node2-1-1-2: 0.5000 (−0.0034)
- SGDR cycle progression: cycle 1→0.4916, cycle 2→0.5056, cycle 3→0.5101, cycle 4→0.5004
- Val/train loss ratio: 1.74× (epoch 80) → 2.54× (epoch 230)

**Key Issues**
- Test F1 below sibling despite higher val_f1 ceiling
- SWA degradation: temperature=3.0 produces near-uniform weights [0.0496–0.0514] across 20 checkpoints with narrow val_f1 range [0.4978, 0.5101], averaging cycle 3 peak with 9 overfitted checkpoints
- Architectural capacity ceiling at epoch ~80–100 with progressive overfitting thereafter

children.name: node2-1-1-3-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Reduced backbone_lr from default to 1e-5, shifting val_f1 peak from epoch 80 (cycle 3) to epoch 41 (cycle 2)
- Improved val/train loss ratio at best epoch from 1.74x to 1.51x, indicating better early generalization
- Adjusted SWA parameters: top_k=6, threshold=0.505, temperature=10.0, start_epoch=40

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.4979 (vs parent 0.4966, sibling node2-1-1-2's 0.5000, tree-best ~0.5182)
- Best val_f1=0.5122 at epoch 41 (vs parent 0.5101 at epoch 80)
- Training stopped at epoch 101 (patience=60)
- Val/train loss ratio at best epoch: ~1.51x

**Key Issues**
- SWA checkpoint schedule (start=40, every=3) missed the actual best checkpoint at epoch 41
- Only 1 of 21 periodic checkpoints qualified for SWA (epoch 88, val_f1=0.5075) due to aggressive threshold=0.505
- SWA "ensemble" replaced best checkpoint with single inferior checkpoint, degrading test performance
- Systemic mismatch between SWA design (calibrated for cycle-3 peaks) and actual training dynamics (cycle-2 peak at lower backbone_lr)
- Expected test F1 of ~0.50–0.51 from best checkpoint was lost to SWA post-processing

children.name: node2-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Complete pivot from AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA architecture back to frozen STRING_GNN backbone (5.43M params)
- 6-layer deep residual bilinear MLP head (hidden=512, expand=4, bilinear rank=256, ~7M trainable params)
- Mild class-weighted focal loss (γ=2.0, weights=[down=1.5, neutral=0.8, up=2.5])
- AdamW with cosine LR schedule (lr=5e-4, total_steps=6600, warmup=50)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: **0.5011** vs parent 0.4234 (+0.0777)
- New MCTS tree-best (surpasses previous best node1-2-3 F1=0.4969 by +0.0042)
- Two-phase training: F1=0.2825 (epoch 0) → 0.4948 (epoch 20) → 0.5011 peak (epoch 51)
- Early stopping at epoch 101 (patience=50)
- Val-to-test F1 gap: 0.0000 (0.5011 vs 0.5011)
- Val/train loss ratio at best: 2.62× (epoch 51), final: 5.27×

**Key Issues**
- Frozen backbone limitation: pre-computed static PPI embeddings cannot adapt to perturbation-to-DEG mapping task
- No explicit graph propagation at inference time, capping representational power
- Post-epoch-51 overfitting (val/train loss ratio increased from 2.62× to 5.27×)

children.name: node2-1-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Partial backbone fine-tuning: last 2 GCN layers + post_mp (~45K params at LR=1e-5)
- Bilinear head rank increased from 256 to 512 (6-layer residual MLP, hidden=512, ~11M head params)
- Stronger class weights: [down=2.0, neutral=0.5, up=4.0] vs [1.5, 0.8, 2.5]
- Two-group AdamW: backbone LR=1e-5, head LR=5e-4

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5016 vs parent 0.5011 (+0.0005)
- Best validation F1: 0.5016 at epoch 22
- Early stopping at epoch 72 (patience=50 exhausted)
- Validation F1 oscillated 0.467–0.498 after epoch 22, never exceeded peak
- Training loss declined from 0.086 to 0.050

**Key Issues**
- Negligible performance gain despite three simultaneous changes
- Overfitting after epoch 22 (train loss declined while val F1 stagnated)
- Secondary improvement phase (parent epoch 51) absent; peak arrived 29 epochs earlier
- Partial backbone fine-tuning ineffective with 1,416 samples (frozen and fine-tuned converge to ~F1=0.501)
- Architectural ceiling of STRING_GNN bilinear head approach
- Lack of cell-line-specific or sequence-level biological features in gene representations
- Independent per-gene bilinear scoring cannot capture cross-gene co-regulatory interactions

children.name: node2-1-2-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Replaced STRING_GNN PPI topology backbone with frozen ESM2-650M protein language model (precomputed mean-pooled sequence embeddings, 1280-dim)
- Maintained proven 6-layer residual bilinear MLP head architecture (rank=256, hidden=512, expand=4, dropout=0.2)
- Used class-weighted focal loss (gamma=2.0, weights=[1.5, 0.8, 2.5])

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.4359 (-0.0657 vs parent node2-1-2-1 F1=0.5016, -0.0652 vs node2-1-2 F1=0.5011)
- Best validation F1=0.4359 achieved at epoch 32
- Training ran for 83 epochs with AdamW (lr=5e-4, wd=1e-3, cosine annealing, warmup=100, patience=50)
- Val loss reached global minimum at epoch 6 (0.172) then increased monotonically to 0.286 by epoch 82
- Train loss declined from 0.111 to 0.056; final val/train loss ratio=5.11× (substantial overfitting)
- Below even AIDO.Cell lineage baseline (F1=0.4234)

**Key Issues**
- ESM2 protein sequence embeddings (capturing biochemical structure and evolutionary context) provide insufficient task-discriminative signal for predicting transcriptional perturbation responses
- PPI topology directly encodes co-regulatory network structure more predictive of co-regulation patterns than protein sequence identity alone
- Mean-pooling over full protein sequences (up to 1024 tokens) dilutes domain-specific functional signals
- Insufficient training samples (1,416) for head to generalize from frozen ESM2 embeddings; very early val loss divergence at epoch 6 indicates embedding inadequacy

children.name: node2-1-2-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Multi-modal fusion: combined frozen STRING_GNN PPI topology embeddings (256-dim) + frozen ESM2-650M protein sequence embeddings (1280-dim) with per-modality LayerNorm before concatenation (1536-dim fused representation)
- Modified 4-layer residual bilinear MLP head (hidden=512, rank=256, dropout=0.25, increased from dropout=0.2 in parent)
- Maintained class-weighted focal loss (gamma=2.0, weights=[1.5, 0.8, 2.5]) and AdamW optimizer (lr=5e-4, wd=1e-3)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4851 (parent: 0.4359) → +0.0492 improvement
- Best val F1: 0.4851 at epoch 80
- Early stopping triggered at epoch 130
- Training: 8 GPUs, global batch size 128, cosine annealing (warmup=100, total=6600 steps)
- Training dynamics: rapid F1 improvement 0.252→0.485 (epochs 0-80), then plateau at 0.473±0.004
- Overfitting progression: train/val loss ratio increased from 5.2× (epoch 80) to 11.3× (epoch 130)
- Val loss increased monotonically 0.251→0.339 (epochs 80-130)
- Early stopping at ~22% of LR schedule (step ~1440 of 6600)
- Still 0.0165 below best STRING_GNN-only nodes (0.5011-0.5016)

**Key Issues**
- Dimensional asymmetry: ESM2 (1280-dim) is 5× larger than STRING_GNN (256-dim), causing ESM2 to dominate fused representation despite per-modality LayerNorm
- Dimensional imbalance dilutes STRING_GNN PPI topology signal rather than adding complementary information
- Early overfitting: val loss diverged while train loss continued decreasing
- Suboptimal LR utilization: model stopped training before benefiting from low-LR fine-tuning phase of cosine schedule

children.name: node2-1-2-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Switched from partially fine-tuned STRING_GNN backbone to frozen backbone with pre-computed embeddings
- Replaced two-group AdamW with MuonWithAuxAdam optimizer (Muon lr=0.005, AdamW lr=5e-4)
- Replaced cosine annealing with SGDR warm restarts (T_0=600 steps ≈ 27 epochs, T_mult=1)
- Added quality-filtered exponential SWA (top-25, threshold=0.497, temperature=2.5)
- Set dropout=0.45, label smoothing=0.07, weight decay=2e-3
- Increased early stopping patience from 50 to 200

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5070 vs parent 0.5016 (+0.0054)
- Best val_f1: 0.5070 at epoch 17 vs parent 0.5016 at epoch 22
- Training duration: 217 epochs vs parent 72 epochs
- Train loss: 0.037 (epoch 217) vs parent 0.050
- Val_loss: 0.342 (epoch 217) — severe overfitting gap
- SGDR cycle performance degradation: cycle 1 peak 0.5070 → cycle 2: 0.4979 → cycle 8: 0.4734
- SWA pool size: only 4/68 checkpoints qualified (val_f1 ≥ 0.497), all in first cycle

**Key Issues**
- SGDR T_0 strategy mismatch: used T_0=600 steps (27-epoch cycles) instead of T_0=20 steps (1–2 epoch micro-cycles) from the reference best-tree node (F1=0.5182)
- SWA threshold too restrictive (0.497) resulting in critically undersized pool of 4 checkpoints
- Severe and progressive overfitting: train loss continuous decrease to 0.037 while val_loss climbed to 0.342
- Strictly descending val_f1 staircase across SGDR cycles (opposite of expected ascending pattern)
- Best checkpoint occurred extremely early (epoch 17) with no recovery in subsequent 200 epochs

children.name: node2-1-2-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Increased bilinear rank from 256 to 512 in 6-layer residual MLP head (~11M trainable params vs ~7M)
- Strengthened class-weighted focal loss weights from [1.5, 0.8, 2.5] to [2.0, 0.5, 4.0]
- Replaced AdamW with MuonWithAuxAdam (Muon lr=0.005 for 2D weights, AdamW lr=5e-4 for embeddings)
- Recalibrated cosine LR schedule total_steps from 6600 to 1650 (150-epoch window)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4925 (vs parent 0.5011, Δ = -0.0086)
- Val F1 peak: 0.4923 at epoch 12 (vs parent peak 0.5011 at epoch 51)
- Early stopping: epoch 62 (patience=50)
- Final train/val loss ratio: 0.027 (vs parent 5.27×)
- Train loss: 0.354 → 0.017 (96% reduction)
- Val loss: 0.193 → 0.655 (3.4× increase)

**Key Issues**
- Extreme early-peak overfitting: val F1 peaked at epoch 12 then monotonically declined to 0.4617
- Severe memorization: train/val loss ratio of 0.027 indicates near-total overfitting
- Muon lr=0.005 too aggressive for rank=512 architecture on 1,416 training samples
- Doubling rank from 256 to 512 amplified Muon's tendency to memorize
- Secondary improvement phase never emerged: cosine decay at only 8% when peak occurred

children.name: node2-1-2-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Reduced Muon lr from 0.005 to 0.002 for 2D weight matrices
- Extended cosine warmup from 50 to 150 steps (total_steps=1200)
- Targeted best-epoch delay from ~12 to ~30-50

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4932 (+0.0007 vs parent's 0.4925)
- Best val F1: 0.4932 at epoch 17 (vs parent's epoch 12)
- Training duration: 68 epochs (patience=50 triggered from epoch 17)
- Val F1 trajectory: declined from 0.493 to 0.470 during post-peak training
- Train loss: dropped from 0.094 to 0.030
- Train/val loss divergence: 14× ratio at termination

**Key Issues**
- Extended warmup provided only marginal delay (5 epochs vs targeted 18-38 epoch shift)
- Secondary improvement phase driven by cosine LR decay never materialized
- Model already in overfitting regime before meaningful LR reduction occurred
- Rank=512 (~9M parameters) + Muon orthogonalized momentum is structurally overparameterized for 1,416 training samples
- Lower lr=0.002 only partially mitigated fundamental capacity-optimization mismatch

children.name: node2-1-2-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Replaced AdamW with MuonWithAuxAdam (Muon lr=0.002 for hidden matrices, AdamW lr=5e-4 for embeddings)
- Increased class weights from [down=1.5, neutral=0.8, up=2.5] to [down=2.0, neutral=0.5, up=4.0]
- Reduced cosine LR schedule: total_steps from 6600 to 1650, warmup from 50 to 100 steps

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4961 (parent: 0.5011) → -0.0050 regression
- Val F1 peak: 0.4960 at epoch 26
- Training duration: 77 epochs before early stopping (parent: 101 epochs)
- Train loss: 0.034, Val loss: 0.399 (val/train ratio: 11.7×)
- Overfitting onset: epoch 11

**Key Issues**
- Muon lr=0.002 over-corrected: fell below AdamW baseline performance (tree evidence: Muon lr=0.005 at rank=256 yields F1=0.5023)
- Heavy class weights [2.0, 0.5, 4.0] amplified gradient variance for rare up-class (3.0% frequency), causing overfitting before LR decay
- Expected secondary improvement phase from calibrated LR decay never emerged
- Val loss rose continuously after epoch 11 while train loss dropped, confirming moderate-to-severe overfitting

children.name: node2-1-2-3-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Increased Muon LR from 0.002 to 0.005 for 2D hidden matrices
- Replaced calibrated cosine LR schedule with SGDR warm restarts (T_0=600 steps, T_mult=1.5, warmup=150 steps)
- Increased dropout from default to 0.3
- Increased model size from ~7M to ~14.8M trainable params
- Reduced class weights from [2.0, 0.5, 4.0] to milder [1.5, 0.8, 2.5]
- Added quality-filtered post-hoc SWA (threshold=0.497, top-k=10)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4923 (parent: 0.4961, Δ: −0.0038)
- Val F1 peak: 0.4921 at epoch 28 (parent peak: 0.4960 at epoch 26)
- Training duration: 108 epochs (parent: 77 epochs, early stopped)
- Train/val loss ratio at final epoch: 15.30× (parent: severe overfitting from epoch 11)
- SGDR restart at epoch 68 (step 759) produced brief bounce to 0.482, then continued overfitting
- SWA checkpoints collected: 0 (threshold 0.497 never reached; model max=0.4921)

**Key Issues**
- Mild class weights [1.5, 0.8, 2.5] incompatible with Muon lr=0.005: tree evidence shows Muon lr=0.005 + strong weights [2.0, 0.5, 4.0] achieves F1=0.5023, but same LR with mild weights achieves only 0.4921 (−0.0100 deficit)
- SWA threshold 0.497 set above model's empirical performance ceiling (0.4921), resulting in zero checkpoints collected and no SWA benefit
- Weight decay 1e-3 insufficient to prevent 15.30× train/val loss ratio overfitting
- Catastrophic overfitting: train loss continued dropping while val loss rose, with no secondary recovery phase after SGDR restart

children.name: node2-1-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Switched from AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA to partially fine-tuned STRING_GNN backbone (frozen mps.0–5 pre-computed as buffer; trainable mps.6, mps.7, post_mp; ~198K trainable backbone params)
- Replaced large MLP head with rank=512 6-layer residual bilinear MLP head (~16.9M params)
- Added strong class weighting to focal loss: [down=2.0, neutral=0.5, up=4.0]
- Implemented two LR groups with 10× separation (backbone=5e-5, head=5e-4)
- Set cosine annealing with total_steps=6600, warmup=100, patience=50

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5047 vs 0.42337 (+0.0813, ~19.2% relative improvement)
- Achieved new tree-best F1, surpassing sibling node2-1-2 (0.5011) by +0.0036
- Best val F1=0.5047 at epoch 32
- Training loss: 0.080→0.041 over epochs 32–82
- Validation loss: 0.210→0.299 over same period (overfitting after peak)
- Training stopped at epoch 83 due to patience

**Key Issues**
- LR schedule miscalibration: total_steps=6600 designed for ~600 epochs, but training stopped at epoch 83, causing cosine LR to remain essentially flat (5e-4→4.82e-4)

- Insufficient regularization for high-capacity head (16.9M params on 1,416 samples): dropout=0.2, weight_decay=1e-3 inadequate
- No secondary improvement phase (unlike node2-1-2's slow post-peak gains)

tree.name: node3
tree.memory:
**Technical Implementation**
- Model: AIDO.Cell-3M (hidden_size=128, 6 transformer layers)
- Fine-tuning: QKV-only direct fine-tuning
- Optimizer: Muon (lr_muon=0.02, lr_adam=3e-4) with ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler
- Pooling: Learnable single-query attention pooling over all 19,264 gene positions → 128-dim vector
- Prediction head: Linear(128→512→6640×3)
- Loss: Cross-entropy with class weights [12.28, 1.12, 33.33], label_smoothing=0.1
- Training: 38 epochs with early stopping (patience=20)

**Results & Metrics**
- Best val_f1: 0.1696 at epoch 17
- Test F1: 0.1693 (near-perfect checkpoint selection, no val-to-test gap)
- 8-epoch warm-up phase: val_f1 stagnated at ~0.063
- Three LR reductions: 0.02→0.01→0.005→0.0025
- Validation loss: monotonically increased from 1.40 to 1.50

**Key Issues**
- Severe representation bottleneck: collapsing 19,264 gene states into single 128-dim vector
- Single-query attention pooling discards per-gene positional information
- AIDO.Cell-3M minimal hidden size insufficient for 6,640×3 structured prediction
- Loss-level overfitting: validation loss rose throughout training despite F1 peak
- Non-monotonic dynamics with irreversible decay after epoch 17

children.name: node3-1
children.memory:

- AIDO.Cell-3M → AIDO.Cell-10M (hidden_size 128→256, layers 6→8)
- Attention pooling replaced with direct gene-position extraction (uses perturbed gene's index into `last_hidden_state`)
- Prediction head: Linear(128→512→6640×3) → Linear(256→1024→6640×3)
- Class weights refined: [12.28, 1.12, 33.33] → [10.91, 1.0, 29.62]
- Label smoothing: 0.1 → 0.05
- Muon lr_muon: 0.02 → 0.01
- Early stopping patience: 20 → 25

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**

- Best val_f1: 0.1696 (epoch 17) → 0.3850 (epoch 69)
- Training epochs: 38 → 95 (patience stop)
- Train/val loss gap at best epoch: 0.18 → 0.83
- Checkpoint selection: near-perfect on both nodes (val≈test)

**Key Issues**
- Over-parameterization: 22.26M trainable params on 1,416 training samples (~15,700 params/sample)
- Validation loss monotonically increased (1.14→1.49) while training loss decreased (1.17→0.66)
- Severe overfitting at calibration level (final val-train loss gap ~0.83)
- Short of Node 4 STRING_GNN baseline: 0.3853 vs 0.4258

children.name: node3-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Switched from AIDO.Cell-10M to AIDO.Cell-100M backbone (hidden_size=640, 18 layers vs. 256, 8 layers)
- Replaced QKV-only direct fine-tuning (22.26M params, 70% trainable) with LoRA adapters on all QKV matrices (~2.7M params, rank=8, alpha=16)
- Changed optimizer from Muon to pure AdamW (lr_backbone=1e-4, lr_head=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01)
- Switched from weighted cross-entropy to focal loss (gamma=2.0, label_smoothing=0.05, same class_weights [10.91, 1.0, 29.62])
- Widened prediction head from Linear(256→1024→6640×3) to Linear(640→2048→6640×3) with dropout=0.2
- Inherited gene-position extraction architecture from Node 3-1

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4096 vs. parent 0.3853 (+0.0243 absolute improvement)
- Best val F1: 0.4094 at epoch 12 (parent: 0.3850 at epoch 69)
- Training duration: 43 epochs before early stopping vs. parent 95 epochs (patience 30 vs. 25)
- Val-to-test generalization gap: ≈0.0002 (near-perfect checkpoint selection, similar to parent)
- Training dynamics: rapid improvement phase (epochs 0–12, val F1: 0.3095→0.4094), then 30-epoch plateau (val F1: 0.3804–0.4085, mean=0.4020, std=0.006)
- Final training loss: 0.0172; final val loss: 1.4698 (val-train loss gap ~1.45)
- Performance gap vs. Node 4 (STRING_GNN): 0.4258 – 0.4096 = 0.0162 F1

**Key Issues**
- Early plateau at val F1 ≈ 0.41 after only 12 epochs, followed by 30-epoch stagnation despite continued training
- LoRA rank=8 constrains backbone updates to 8-dimensional subspace of 640×640 weight space (2.7M trainable vs. parent's 22.26M)
- Sparse input encoding {perturbed_gene: 1.0, all_others: -1.0} is out-of-distribution relative to AIDO.Cell-100M's pre-training regime
- Val loss divergence (0.7868→1.4698) while training loss collapsed to 0.0172, indicating calibration deterioration despite stable argmax predictions
- Falls short of STRING_GNN (Node 4) by 0.0162 F1, which encodes protein-protein interaction topology absent from transcriptomic backbone

children.name: node3-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Bimodal fusion architecture: AIDO.Cell-100M (640-dim) + STRING_GNN perturbation-conditioned embeddings (256-dim via cond_emb injection)
- Increased LoRA rank from 8 to 16 on QKV+FFN (~10.8M trainable params vs parent 2.7M)
- Concatenation to 896-dim representation through Linear(896→1536→6640×3) prediction head
- Learning rate adjustment: lr_backbone=5e-5, lr_head=2e-4 (vs parent 1e-4/3e-4)
- Added 10-epoch linear warmup with LambdaLR cosine annealing scheduler

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4574 vs 0.4096 = +0.0478 (+11.7%)
- Best val F1: 0.4574 at epoch 35 vs 0.4094 at epoch 12
- Val-to-test gap: 0.0 (perfect checkpoint selection)
- Val F1 stable in [0.438, 0.457] range post-epoch 22
- Trainable parameters: ~44M total (~32M head params, ~22,500 params/sample)
- Training loss: 0.008 (collapsed)
- Validation loss: 1.225 at epoch 70 (val/train ratio ≈154×)
- Underperforms node1-2 (frozen STRING_GNN alone): 0.4574 vs 0.4912 (−0.034)

**Key Issues**
- Severe calibration overfitting: val loss diverged to 154× train loss while val F1 plateaued
- AIDO.Cell OOD input limitation: sparse input {perturbed_gene: 1.0, others: -1.0} produces contextually uninformative embeddings that dilute STRING_GNN signal
- Head over-parameterization: ~32M head params (~22,500 params/sample) exceeds dataset capacity (1,416 samples)
- Bimodal fusion underperforms pure STRING_GNN baseline (node1-2 F1=0.4912)

children.name: node3-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Frozen STRING_GNN backbone (replacing parent's AIDO.Cell+STRING_GNN fusion)
- Inductive Conditioning MLP: LN→Linear(256→512)→GELU→Dropout(0.1)→Linear(512→256, near-zero init)
- Perturbation-specific additive delta on frozen PPI embedding
- 6-layer residual MLP + bilinear head (rank=256) from node1-2
- AdamW optimizer: lr=5e-4, wd=1e-3
- Focal loss: gamma=1.0
- Step-based cosine annealing: T_max=1800 steps

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4671 vs parent 0.4574 → +0.0097 improvement
- Gap to MCTS best (node1-2): -0.0241 (F1=0.4912)
- Peak val F1: 0.4670 at epoch 10
- Val F1 trajectory: 0.4670 (ep10) → 0.4186 (ep20) → 0.4635 (ep60)
- Val loss: 0.49→0.84 (monotonic divergence)
- Train loss: 0.133 at epoch 60
- Train/val loss ratio: 6.34× (severe overfitting)
- LR schedule: T_max=1800 ≈ 41 epochs, LR at 65% of peak at epoch 60 (wrapped, not decayed)

**Key Issues**
- **LayerNorm on `cond_delta` nullifies near-zero init**: unconditional rescaling to unit variance enables cond_mlp to immediately dominate representation
- Rapid memorization of 1,416 training perturbations within 10 epochs
- Incorrect T_max: 1800 steps ≈ 41 epochs (not 120–150), causing LR wraparound instead of secondary decay phase
- Insufficient dropout (0.1) to prevent premature overfitting
- Cond_mlp overwhelms frozen STRING_GNN signal without gating control

children.name: node3-1-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Replaced LayerNorm on `cond_delta` with learnable scalar gate (init=0.01) to preserve near-zero initialization strategy
- Simplified conditioning module: removed 2-layer MLP to single Linear(256→256, std=0.01) with LayerNorm on input only, increased Dropout(0.1→0.35)
- Corrected cosine annealing T_max from 1800 to 8800 steps to enable proper LR decay through full training
- Increased focal loss gamma from 1.0 to 2.0, added label_smoothing=0.05, tuned class_weights=[10.91, 1.0, 29.62]
- Applied two-group weight decay: main wd=1e-3, conditioning wd=5e-2

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4837 (+0.0166 vs parent 0.4671; still -0.0075 vs node1-2 best 0.4912)
- Validation best F1: 0.4837 at epoch 102 (vs parent peak 0.4670 at epoch 10)
- Training extended to 152 epochs with EarlyStopping (vs parent collapse at epoch 10)
- LR at epoch 152: ~6.7×10⁻⁵ (proper cosine decay; vs parent at 65% of peak due to T_max restart bug)
- Val/train loss ratio: 15.26× at epoch 102 (improved vs parent 6.34× at epoch 10, but persistent overfitting)
- Training phases: epochs 0-10 rapid (F1: 0.31→0.45), epochs 10-75 gradual (0.45→0.48), epochs 76-77 anomaly (train loss spike 0.054→0.094), epochs 78-152 plateau

**Key Issues**
- Conditioning module consistently underperforms pure node1-2 baseline across 5 attempts (node1-2-2: 0.4664; node3-1-1-1-1: 0.4671; node3-1-1-1-1-1: 0.4837 vs node1-2: 0.4912)
- Frozen STRING_GNN PPI-topology embeddings lack perturbation-specific transcriptomic response information for effective conditioning
- Bf16 numerical instability caused train-loss spike at epochs 76-77 (0.054→0.094)
- Persistent overfitting with val/train loss ratio reaching 15.26×

children.name: node3-1-1-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Removed conditioning module entirely (returned to pure pipeline architecture)
- Increased bilinear rank from 256 to 512
- Increased head depth from 6 to 8 residual MLP layers (512-dim, expand=4)
- Increased dropout from 0.2 to 0.3
- Increased weight decay from 1e-3 to 3e-3 (main params) and 5e-3 (out_gene_emb separately)
- Added gradient clipping at clip_val=1.0
- Extended warmup to 100 steps
- Total trainable parameters: ~17M (vs parent's 9M)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.4788 (regression of -0.0049 vs parent 0.4837)
- Regression of -0.0124 vs MCTS best (node1-2: 0.4912)
- Best val F1=0.4788 at epoch 62
- EarlyStopping at epoch 112 (50 epochs after best)
- Val/train loss ratio at best epoch: ~11.5× (worse than node1-2's 3.71×)
- Gradient clipping successfully eliminated bf16 numerical instability (no step-level loss spikes > 0.5)

**Key Issues**
- Only gradient clipping produced genuine improvement; all other changes yielded net negative result
- Capacity-data mismatch: ~17M parameters with only 1,416 training samples (~12,000 parameters per sample)
- Out_gene_emb [6640, 512] = 3.4M params specifically overwhelmed regularization capacity
- Doubled parameter count prevented increased regularization (dropout 0.3, wd 3e-3) from improving generalization

children.name: node3-1-1-1-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Reverted bilinear rank from 512 to 256 and head depth from 8 to 6 residual layers (back to node1-2's proven configuration)
- Reduced dropout from 0.3 to 0.2
- Replaced two-group AdamW (lr=5e-4) with hybrid optimizer: Muon (lr=0.02) for hidden weight matrices, AdamW for output heads and embeddings
- Extended T_max from 8800 to 13200 cosine steps (300 epochs target)
- Reduced weight decay: from 3e-3/5e-3 back to node1-2's 1e-3

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4626 (regression of -0.0162 vs parent's 0.4788)
- MCTS best comparison: -0.0286 below node1-2 (0.4912)
- Best val F1: 0.4624 at epoch 20 (vs parent: 0.4788 at epoch 62)
- Early stopping at epoch 70 (patience=50), having traversed only 23.3% of T_max=13200
- Val/train loss ratio at best epoch: 3.37x (vs parent's 11.5x — healthier)
- Val/train loss ratio at termination: 7.83x
- Final training loss: 0.059

**Key Issues**
- Structural mismatch: Muon's high initial LR (0.02, 40× AdamW) drives rapid convergence to generalization plateau by epoch 20, while LR remained at 87% of max — negating the intended extended low-LR training phase
- Val F1 stagnated in 0.440-0.462 range after epoch 20 while training loss continued decreasing (overfitting)
- Early stopping triggered at epoch 70 before reaching the cosine decay regime that was intended to provide gradual convergence
- Only 23.3% of T_max schedule was utilized, meaning the 300-epoch design was never realized

children.name: node3-1-1-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- LayerNorm removal on cond_delta to improve gradient flow
- Corrected T_max calibration from 4400 to 3300 steps
- Reduced bilinear head rank from 1024 to 512
- Changed class weights from [10.91, 1.0, 29.62] to [2.0, 0.5, 4.0]
- Increased cond_mlp dropout from 0.2 to 0.3
- Increased cond_mlp weight decay from 1e-4 to 1e-2
- Reduced head LR from 5e-4 to 3e-4

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5003 (+0.0429 vs parent 0.4574)
- Best val F1: 0.5002 at epoch 23 (val-to-test gap: +0.0001)
- Training: 84 epochs on 2 GPUs, early stopping at epoch 83 (patience=60)
- Train loss: 0.471 → 0.043 (monotonic decrease)
- Val loss: 0.236 (min, epoch 4) → 0.546 (epoch 83)
- Train/val loss ratio: 12.7× (severe overfitting)
- Cosine schedule incomplete: 55% complete at early stopping (lr_head at 43% of peak)

**Key Issues**
- **Critical failure**: Inductive conditioning mechanism inoperative — cond_gate oscillated near zero (range −0.007 to +0.012, final ~0.001) throughout all 84 epochs
- Gradient stagnation trap: near-zero cond_fc2 initialization → near-zero cond_delta → near-zero gradient for cond_gate
- High weight decay (wd=1e-2) on gate parameter prevents escape from zero fixed point
- Conditioning MLP contributed zero to predictions; node functionally equivalent to frozen STRING_GNN + bilinear head
- Severe overfitting: val loss diverged 2.3× from minimum while train loss collapsed
- Architecture underperforms node1-2-3-2 (frozen STRING_GNN + rank=512, no conditioning) by only +0.0007 despite added conditioning complexity

children.name: node3-1-1-1-2-1
children.memory:

- Train loss: 0.486 → 0.099 (monotonic decline)
- Highly unstable early-epoch behavior (epoch 0 val_f1=0.272)
- Validation oscillated in 0.478–0.495 range after peak
- Severe overfitting after best epoch

**Key Issues**
- Cross-lineage instability of partial backbone fine-tuning: node3 lineage context produced suboptimal early-epoch embeddings converging into local minimum ~0.005 below frozen-backbone parent
- Backbone destabilization evidenced by epoch 0 val_f1=0.272 collapse
- Secondary LR-decay improvement phase did not materialize
- Significant overfitting with train/val loss divergence after best epoch
- Fragility of backbone fine-tuning confirmed: same blueprint achieved F1=0.5047 in node2-1-3 lineage, but all node2-1-3 children also regressed from tree best

children.name: node3-1-1-1-2-1-1
children.memory:

- Fully frozen STRING_GNN backbone (complete precomputed pass: emb+mps.0-7+post_mp → static buffer [18870, 256])
- Enhanced class-weighted focal loss: [2.5, 0.5, 5.0] (vs parent [2.0, 0.5, 4.0])
- Label smoothing=0.05 added (vs parent none)

- wd=1.5e-3 (vs parent 1.0e-3)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4928 (parent: 0.4952, regression: −0.0024)
- Vs grandparent frozen baseline (node3-1-1-1-2): −0.0075 (0.4928 vs 0.5003)
- Vs tree best (node2-1-3): −0.0119 (0.4928 vs 0.5047)
- Best epoch: 22 (val_f1=0.4928, val_loss=0.2998, train_loss=0.1146)
- Training duration: 73 epochs (0–72) before early stopping

- Frozen backbone reversion failed to recover performance (unexpected regression vs grandparent 0.5003)
- Severe overfitting: val_loss increased 0.2998→0.5156 while train_loss decreased 0.1146→0.0601 (final gap=0.4555)
- val_f1 remained flat (0.479–0.483) post-peak while val_loss rose — confidence miscalibration despite stable argmax boundaries
- Compounding negative interaction: enhanced class weights [2.5, 0.5, 5.0] + label smoothing=0.05 was counterproductive
- Grandparent baseline used [2.0, 0.5, 4.0] without smoothing and achieved 0.5003 — confirming the combined changes were detrimental

children.name: node3-1-1-1-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Partial STRING_GNN fine-tuning: mps.6+mps.7+post_mp frozen via buffer, trainable tail at backbone_lr=1e-5
- 6-block rank-512 deep bilinear MLP head (dropout=0.45) replacing Linear(896→1536→6640×3) architecture

- SGDR warm restarts: T_0=20 epochs, T_mult=2
- Post-hoc quality-filtered SWA: threshold=0.497, top-k=15, temperature=3.0

- Best val F1: 0.5093 at epoch 93 vs 0.4574 at epoch 35

- Early stopping at epoch 243 (patience=150)

- Progressive overfitting: train/val loss ratio 2.14× at epoch-93 peak, 2.83× at training end
- Gap to tree best (0.5182): −0.0089

**Key Issues**
- SGDR staircase truncated after epoch 93 — model unable to escape local optimum despite multiple restarts
- SWA pool quality: top-15 checkpoints all below best single checkpoint, rendering SWA ineffective
- Architecture saturation at F1≈0.509 with current bilinear rank-512 + SGDR configuration
- Moderate overfitting: 150 stagnation epochs increased train/val loss divergence without F1 gain

children.name: node3-1-1-1-3-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Shortened SGDR cycles: T_0=15 epochs (vs 20), T_mult=1.5 (vs 2) — created more restart opportunities
- Tightened SWA strategy: threshold=0.505 (vs 0.497), top-k=8 (vs 15), temperature=10.0 (vs 3.0)
- Modified frozen buffer strategy: mps.0–5 frozen as precomputed buffer, mps.6+mps.7+post_mp trainable at lr=1e-5 (vs parent: mps.6+mps.7+post_mp frozen via buffer, trainable tail at lr=1e-5)
- Adjusted focal loss class_weights: [2.0, 0.5, 4.0] (parent values not explicitly stated)

- Test F1: 0.5142 (+0.0049 vs parent 0.5093)
- Best validation F1: 0.5142 at epoch 149 (parent: 0.5093 at epoch 93)
- SGDR cycles: 5 cycles complete with staircase gains through cycle 5 (parent: plateaued after cycle 2 at epoch 93)
- SWA pool: 70 qualifying checkpoints (val_f1 ≥ 0.505), top-8 averaging with near-uniform weights (0.123–0.130)
- SWA effectiveness: no measurable gain over best single checkpoint (temperature=10.0 produced uniform weights due to score differences <0.006)

**Key Issues**
- Performance ceiling at F1≈0.514: cycles 3–4 showed slight regression from cycle 2 (0.5117), cycle 6+ showed clear decline
- Model converges to local optimum that repeated LR restarts cannot escape significantly
- SWA ineffective: high temperature (10.0) with tiny score differences produced uniform weights, negating selective averaging benefit
- Progressive overfitting pattern: train/val loss ratio increased from 2.14× at epoch 93 peak to 2.83× at training end (inherited from parent dynamics)

children.name: node3-1-1-1-3-1-1
children.memory:

children.name: node3-1-1-1-3-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Replaced temperature-based SWA with explicit cycle-peak ensemble (averaging top-4 SGDR-cycle-peak checkpoints with equal weights)
- Modified SGDR parameters: T_0=25 epochs (from 20), T_mult=1.2 (from 1.3)
- Adjusted learning rates: backbone_lr=3e-5 (from 1e-5), Muon lr=0.006 (from 0.007)
- Changed SGDR eta_min to 1e-5 (from parent's implicit baseline)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.5099 (parent: 0.5157, regression: −0.0058)
- Best val_f1=0.5105 at epoch 77 (cycle 3 peak)
- Training stopped at epoch 277 (patience=200)
- Six SGDR cycles completed with peaks: 0.5099 (ep 48), 0.5105 (ep 77), 0.5089 (ep 91), 0.5091 (ep 149), 0.5060 (ep 197), 0.5004 (ep 255)
- Cycle-peak ensemble (top-4 val_f1: 0.5105, 0.5099, 0.5091, 0.5089) yielded test score 0.5099 (−0.0006 vs single best checkpoint)
- All top-4 checkpoints cluster within 0.0016 val_f1 range with insufficient diversity

**Key Issues**
- Muon lr reduction from 0.007 to 0.006 weakened cycle 2 peak from 0.5134 (parent) to 0.5099 (−0.0035)
- Hypothesized extended staircase to cycles 4–5 never materialized — instead, cycles 4–7 declined below cycle 3
- Ensemble failed to add value because checkpoint pool lacks sufficient diversity and performance (all peaks <0.511)
- Cycle-peak ensemble technique is architecturally sound but requires higher-performing checkpoint pool to provide measurable benefit

children.name: node3-1-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Increased LoRA rank from 8 to 32 (QKV-only, alpha=64, ~44M trainable parameters including head vs 2.7M all-layer)
- Added mean-expression baseline input (background genes set to per-gene mean expression from 1,416 sparse training samples)
- Changed optimizer schedule: SequentialLR with 10-epoch linear warmup + CosineAnnealingWarmRestarts (T_0=50) vs pure cosine annealing
- Adjusted learning rates: lr_backbone=5e-5, lr_head=2e-4 (vs 1e-4, 3e-4 in parent)
- Added neutral-balanced focal loss: 30% neutral position subsampling per step, gamma=2.0, class_weights=[10.91, 1.0, 29.62]

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Best val F1: 0.4074 at epoch 94 (parent: 0.4094 at epoch 12, Δ=-0.0020)
- Test F1: 0.4074 (parent: 0.4096, Δ=-0.0022)
- Val-to-test gap: 0.0 (parent: 0.0002)
- Training epochs: 130 (parent: 43)
- Train loss at best epoch: 0.0128; val loss: 1.251 (calibration ratio ≈ 98×)
- F1 plateau: [0.39, 0.41] range from epoch 30 onwards
- CosineWarmRestarts at epochs 60 and 110 introduced brief instability without escaping plateau
- Substantially below sibling node3-1-1-1: 0.4574 (Δ=-0.0500)

**Key Issues**
- Mean-expression baseline failed to fix AIDO.Cell OOD input: baseline computed from 1,416 sparse vectors (each with one gene=1.0, others≈-1.0) ≈ -1.0 for most genes, providing virtually no improvement over original sparse representation
- Severe calibration overfitting with train loss=0.0128 vs val loss=1.251 at best epoch
- F1 plateau at ~0.41 from epoch 30 onwards indicates representational ceiling
- LoRA rank=32 (QKV-only) provided no advantage over rank=8, confirming plateau is driven by backbone input constraint rather than optimization capacity
- Persistent AIDO.Cell OOD sparse input limitation is the fundamental bottleneck; no amount of LoRA capacity increase or neutral subsampling can overcome this data mismatch

children.name: node3-1-1-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Pivoted backbone from AIDO.Cell-100M to partial STRING_GNN fine-tuning (mps.6+mps.7+post_mp layers, ~198K trainable backbone params)

- Two-group AdamW optimizer: backbone_lr=5e-5, head_lr=5e-4

- Best validation F1: 0.4899 at epoch 56
- Total epochs trained: 136 (early stopping triggered)
- Train/val loss at best epoch: 0.0641 / 0.2386 (gap=0.175)

- Below best STRING_GNN nodes: node2-1-3 (0.5047), node2-1-2-1 (0.5016), node2-1-2 (0.5011)

children.name: node3-1-1-3
children.memory:

- Head architecture: rank-512 deep residual bilinear MLP with 6 ResBlocks (hidden=512), ~16.9M params

children.name: node3-1-1-3-1
children.memory:

- Device mismatch crash in `apply_swa_and_predict` (`Device should be GPU, got cpu`) when `SingleDeviceStrategy` attempted CPU-resident model execution

children.name: node3-1-1-3-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Lowered SWA threshold from 0.490 to 0.480
- Increased weight decay from 1.5e-3 to 3e-3

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1=0.5036 (identical to parent's 0.5036, no gain)
- Best val_f1=0.5036 at epoch 16 (same as parent)
- SGDR cycle peaks: 0.5036 → 0.4953 → 0.4865 → terminal (progressive degradation)
- Training stopped at epoch 136 via early stopping
- Overfitting gap: train loss 0.039 vs val loss 0.710 at epoch 136 (+0.6713)
- SWA averaging attempt produced catastrophic F1=0.2453

**Key Issues**
- SWA off-by-one bug persists: checkpoint filenames show epoch 16 labeled with val_f1=0.4864 (epoch 15's actual value), confirming best checkpoint excluded from SWA pool
- Multi-cycle SWA averaging with trainable backbone creates incoherent weight configurations: averaging checkpoints across three SGDR cycles with co-evolved backbone and head weights produced F1=0.2453
- Score recovered to 0.5036 only because EvaluateAgent re-ran eval.py from best single checkpoint, not from SWA
- Weight decay increase to 3e-3 produced no measurable delay in overfitting onset
- Fundamental incompatibility between SGDR multi-cycle restarts, trainable backbone, and SWA averaging

children.name: node3-1-1-3-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Fully frozen STRING_GNN backbone (all layers precomputed as static `full_embs_buffer [18870, 256]`, trainable params reduced from ~90K to ~65K)
- Increased dropout from 0.45 to 0.50 in 6-layer rank-512 deep residual bilinear MLP head
- Added label_smoothing=0.05 to focal loss (gamma=2.0, class weights [2.0, 0.5, 4.0])
- Changed SGDR from step-based (T_0=200 steps) to epoch-based (T_0=20ep, T_mult=2, patience=200)
- MuonWithAuxAdam: backbone lr eliminated (frozen), Muon lr=0.005 for 2D matrices, AdamW lr=5e-4 for 1D params

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5010 (vs parent 0.5036, regression of −0.0026)
- Best val_f1: 0.5011 at epoch 13 (global peak, single checkpoint used for final scoring)
- SWA pool: 8 checkpoints collected (epochs 10–52, val_f1 range 0.4902–0.4998, max below best single)
- Training dynamics: progressive SGDR cycle degradation (Cycle 1: 0.5011 → Cycle 2: 0.4990 → Cycle 3: 0.4880 → Cycle 4: 0.4691)
- Overfitting: train loss 0.033 vs val loss 0.912 at epoch 213 (ratio 27.9×)

**Key Issues**
- Frozen backbone performance ceiling: fixed 256-dim STRING_GNN embeddings limit representational capacity, eliminating task-specific adaptation that parent's partial fine-tuning provided
- SWA off-by-one bug persists: `pl_module._last_val_f1` fix failed in DDP multi-GPU training (checkpoint labels match previous epoch's val_f1), causing below-threshold checkpoint (epoch 22, actual val_f1=0.4857) to be incorrectly included
- SWA pool quality degradation: max SWA checkpoint (0.4998) below best single checkpoint (0.5011)
- Head learning capacity exhausted by epoch 13 with degradation through subsequent SGDR cycles

children.name: node3-1-1-3-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Fine-tuned post_mp layer (nn.Linear(256, 256), ~65K params) alongside bilinear MLP head (parent: fully frozen backbone)
- SWA scheme redesigned: threshold=0.480, every_n=2, top_k=15, temperature=3.0 (parent: 0.490, 3, 15, 2.0)
- Attempted SWA off-by-one fix using `trainer.logged_metrics` (parent used `pl_module._last_val_f1`)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.5029 vs 0.5010 (+0.0019)
- Best checkpoint: epoch 7 (val_f1=0.5029, train_loss=0.1662)
- 28 SWA qualifying checkpoints collected (epochs 10–68, val_f1 0.4848–0.5005)
- SWA model catastrophic failure: near-uniform class probabilities (~0.333/class), F1=0.2458
- Final epoch 207: val_f1=0.4547 vs train_loss=0.0294 (ratio 33.9×)
- SGDR cycle degradation: 0.5029 → 0.4981 → 0.4824 → 0.4656

**Key Issues**
- SWA off-by-one bug persists: all 15 SWA labels match previous epoch's val_f1
- SWA incoherence with trainable post_mp: different head checkpoints trained in different W_t-spaces cause destructive signal cancellation
- Dual failure: (1) SWA with trainable backbone components incoherent without head-only averaging, (2) DDP off-by-one affects both logged_metrics and _last_val_f1 approaches

children.name: node3-1-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Upgraded backbone from AIDO.Cell-10M to AIDO.Cell-100M (256 hidden → 640 hidden, 8 layers → 18 layers)
- Switched from direct QKV fine-tuning to LoRA (rank=16, alpha=32, all 18 layers QKV matrices)
- Added 5-epoch linear warmup before cosine annealing schedule
- Modified prediction head architecture: Linear(256→1024→6640×3) → Linear(640→1536→19920)
- Implemented perturbed-gene knockdown encoding (perturbed gene at 0.0)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.41475 vs 0.3853 (+0.030 improvement)
- Best val F1: 0.41472 at epoch 85
- Training duration: 126 epochs vs 95 epochs (31 more epochs)
- Early stopping triggered later (patience=40 vs patience=25)
- Overfitting pattern more severe: train loss 920→228 (4× reduction) while val loss 767→1131 (+47% increase)

**Key Issues**
- Incomplete knockdown implementation: all other genes at −1.0 (missing sentinel) instead of 1.0 baseline as required
- Single-gene-position extraction bottleneck: extracting one 640-dim vector from 19,266 hidden states to predict 6,640 output positions
- Performance ceiling around F1≈0.41 despite larger backbone and higher LoRA rank
- Warmup phase delayed competitive performance by ~70 epochs compared to sibling node3-1-1
- Severe overfitting: val-train loss gap widened substantially during training

children.name: node3-1-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- LoRA rank increased from 16 to 32 (QKV+FFN, ~6.2M params vs ~3.1M params)
- Input encoding changed from perturbed-gene-at-0.0 with others at -1.0 to full baseline input (all genes at 1.0, perturbed at 0.0)
- Aggregation changed from single-gene extraction to mean-pool over all 19,264 gene hidden states [B, 19264, 640] → [B, 640]
- Head architecture modified from 640→19920 to two-layer 640→1536→19920

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.27749 vs parent 0.41475 (Δ = -0.1373, -33.1%)
- Worst-performing node in AIDO.Cell lineage
- Val F1 oscillated in [0.237, 0.278] with no upward trend; best val_f1=0.2776 at epoch 5
- Train loss: 1043→857 (17.8% reduction), plateau at 857 after epoch 10
- Val loss plateau at 877 after epoch 10
- Early stopping triggered at epoch 40 (patience=35)

**Key Issues**
- Mean-pool aggregation destroys perturbation-specific signal: with all non-perturbed genes at uniform expression=1.0, the perturbed gene contributes only 1/19264 ≈ 0.005% to the mean
- Produces nearly identical cell-state representations for all 1,416 training samples, making perturbation-specific prediction impossible
- Mean-pool aggregation fundamentally incompatible with single-gene perturbation experiments where only one gene differs

children.name: node3-1-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Switched from AIDO.Cell-10M direct QKV fine-tuning (22.26M params, 70.23% trainable) to AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA (rank=16, alpha=32) for parameter-efficient adaptation
- Added triple-stream architecture: (A) perturbed gene position-specific AIDO.Cell embedding, (B) mean-pooled global transcriptome state, (C) frozen STRING_GNN PPI embeddings (256-dim)
- Enhanced input encoding with full multi-gene baseline context (all 19,264 genes at 1.0, perturbed gene at 0.0)
- Changed from weighted cross-entropy to focal loss (gamma=2.0) with same class weights [10.91, 1.0, 29.62]
- Replaced Muon optimizer with AdamW (backbone lr=1e-4, head lr=3e-4)
- Used LayerNorm→Linear(1024)→GELU→Dropout(0.2)→Linear(6640×3) fusion head

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: **0.4579** (+0.0726 vs node3-1's 0.3853; +0.0431 vs node3-1-2's 0.4148)
- Best val F1: 0.4578 at epoch 50 (out of 91 total epochs, early stopping patience=40)
- Best node3-1-x lineage result; converged after epoch 50 with val F1 plateauing at 0.44–0.46 (mean 0.4487)
- Training loss: smooth decrease 0.0313→0.0084 across 90 epochs
- Validation loss: decreased to ~0.026 at epoch 10, rose to ~0.031 by epoch 50 (~3× gap vs training)

**Key Issues**
- Frozen STRING_GNN embeddings cannot adapt to hTERT-RPE1 cell-line context (no learnable adapter)
- MLP fusion head lacks gene-specific output projections (node1-1-3-1-1 achieved 0.4858 with bilinear head)
- Model reached capacity ceiling under current architecture after epoch 50
- Validation overfitting: val loss diverged from training loss by ~3× at epoch 50

children.name: node3-1-3-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Bilinear output head with learnable `gene_embs[6640,256]` replacing MLP head
- STRING_GNN residual adapter (2-layer MLP, zero-initialized)
- Stronger regularization: dropout 0.2→0.3, weight_decay 0.01→0.05
- Reduced lr_head: 3e-4→2e-4

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4369 (vs parent 0.4579, Δ −0.021)
- Best val F1: 0.4370 at epoch 67/107 (vs parent 0.4578 at epoch 50/91)
- Training loss: plateaued at ~0.0162 (vs parent ~0.0084)
- Val loss: 0.025–0.026 range, stable (vs parent divergence to ~0.031)
- Convergence slower: val F1 at epoch 20 = 0.3723 vs parent ~0.419; epoch 40 = 0.4054 vs parent ~0.431

**Key Issues**
- Aggressive uniform weight decay (0.05) on `gene_embs[6640,256]` prevents escape from near-zero initialization, suppressing bilinear head's core mechanism
- Reduced lr_head (2e-4) compounds slow gene_embs development
- Underfitting indicated by higher training loss plateau vs parent (0.0162 vs 0.0084)

children.name: node3-2
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Upgraded backbone from AIDO.Cell-3M (128-dim, 6 layers) to AIDO.Cell-100M (640-dim, 18 layers)
- Replaced learnable attention pooling with direct gene-position extraction (hidden state at perturbed gene's vocab index)
- Switched from direct QKV fine-tuning to LoRA (r=16, alpha=32) on all QKV matrices, reducing trainable parameters from ~15,700 to ~3,900 params/sample
- Changed optimizer from Muon to pure AdamW (backbone lr=1e-4, head lr=3e-4)
- Replaced standard cross-entropy with focal loss (gamma=2.0) and updated class weights to [10.91, 1.0, 29.62]
- Simplified prediction head from 2-layer (128→512→6640×3) to 2-layer (640→2048→6640×3)
- Changed scheduler from ReduceLROnPlateau to CosineAnnealingLR (T_max=100) with 10-epoch warmup

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4096 (vs parent 0.1693) — +142% relative improvement
- Best val F1: 0.4095 at epoch 46 (vs parent 0.1696 at epoch 17)
- Training epochs: 87 (vs parent 38) before early stopping (patience=40 vs parent 20)
- Training loss: decreased from 1.17 to 0.016 (vs parent 0.76→0.34)
- Validation loss: increased monotonically from 0.90 to 5.17 (vs parent 1.40→1.50)
- Val F1 plateau: oscillated 0.405–0.410 from epoch 46 onward (vs parent peak at epoch 17 then decay)

**Key Issues**
- Severe probability calibration overfitting: val loss increased 5.74× (0.90→5.17) while val F1 remained stable
- Synthetic single-gene input bottleneck: only perturbed gene at 1.0, all 19,263 other genes at −1.0 (missing/OOD for AIDO.Cell)
- Gene-position embedding (640-dim) lacks sufficient information to predict 6,640×3 outputs
- Backbone cannot leverage learned co-expression relationships due to −1.0 masking of non-perturbed genes
- LoRA regularization prevented classification collapse (eliminated node3-1's val F1 degradation) but val F1 ceiling persists at ~0.41
- Representation capacity still insufficient relative to tree-best node1-2 (F1=0.4912)

children.name: node3-2-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Realistic knockdown input representation: all 19,264 genes at expression=1.0, perturbed gene at 0.0 (replacing parent's OOD single-gene activation at 1.0 with all others at −1.0)
- 4-block pre-norm deep residual MLP prediction head (640→2560→640, 4 blocks, ~26.3M params) replacing parent's 2-layer head (640→2048→6640×3)
- LoRA rank doubled: r=32, alpha=64 on all QKV matrices across 18 transformer layers vs parent's r=16, alpha=32

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4405 vs parent 0.4096 (+0.0309, +7.5% relative)
- Best val F1: 0.4402 at epoch 32 vs parent 0.4095 at epoch 46
- Training epochs: 73 (early stopping) vs parent 87
- Train loss trajectory: 1.24→0.15 (epochs 0–32), final 0.037
- Val F1 plateau: std=0.0027 over epochs 30–72
- Val/train loss ratio: 92× at final epoch (severe calibration overfitting)
- Gap to tree best (node1-2: 0.4912): −0.0507
- Trainable params: ~28.5M of 100M total

**Key Issues**
- Single-token information bottleneck: extracts only one 640-dim embedding from perturbed gene position in `last_hidden_state`, must encode all perturbation effects across 6,640 output positions
- Missing bilinear gene-to-gene interaction pathway present in superior STRING_GNN nodes (node1-2)
- Catastrophic probability calibration overfitting (val/train loss ratio 92×) despite stable argmax-based F1
- Performance ceiling at ~0.44 F1 attributed to insufficient information capacity of single-token representation

children.name: node3-2-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Attention-weighted pooling over all 19,264 gene tokens conditioned on perturbed gene identity (replacing single-token extraction, ~0.82M new params)
- Head dropout increased from 0.2 to 0.35
- Backbone LoRA learning rate halved from 1e-4 to 5e-5
- Focal loss gamma reduced from 2.0 to 1.0

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4019 (vs parent 0.4405, regression -0.0386 / -8.8%)
- Training: 100 epochs, early stopping triggered (patience=40, best at epoch 59)
- Best val_f1: 0.4021 (closely matches test 0.4019, no val/test gap)
- Final train_loss: 0.826 (vs parent 0.037, 22.3x higher)
- Val/train loss ratio: 1.72x at best (vs parent 12x)

**Key Issues**
- Severe under-optimization: train_loss never converged to well-fit region
- Over-aggressive combined regularization: lower backbone LR, higher dropout, lower focal gamma, and new attention module all simultaneously reduced effective learning signal
- Over-regularization prevented discriminative fitting despite improved probability calibration

children.name: node3-3
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Upgraded from AIDO.Cell-3M (128-dim) to AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA r=16 QKV fine-tuning across all 18 transformer layers
- Replaced single-query attention pooling bottleneck (19,264 genes → 128-dim) with dual-stream architecture: frozen STRING_GNN PPI embeddings (256-dim) + AIDO.Cell embeddings fused via gated projection → 640-dim combined representation
- Upgraded prediction head from simple Linear(128→512→6640×3) to 6-layer residual MLP with bilinear output interaction head (head_dim=512, 6640×512 gene embeddings)
- Changed input from unrealistic profile to realistic multi-gene knockdown: all 19,264 genes at 1.0, perturbed gene at -1.0 (AIDO.Cell "missing" token)
- Switched from standard cross-entropy to focal cross-entropy (γ=2.0) with inverse-frequency class weights [10.91, 1.0, 29.62]
- Changed optimizer from Muon (lr_muon=0.02, lr_adam=3e-4) to AdamW (backbone lr=1e-4, head lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01) with CosineAnnealingLR (10-epoch warmup)

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4797 vs 0.1693 (+0.310 improvement)
- Val F1 peak: 0.4795 (epoch 35) vs 0.1696 (epoch 17)
- Early stopping at epoch 75 (patience triggered) vs epoch 38
- Training loss converged to 0.044 while validation loss diverged from 0.786 (epoch 6) to 3.004 (epoch 75) — 4× gap indicating calibration overfitting
- Test F1 (0.4797) closely matches best val F1 (0.4795), confirming good checkpoint selection generalization

**Key Issues**
- Calibration overfitting: 6-layer residual head (26M+ parameters) becomes overconfident on training data while val loss rises dramatically
- Frozen STRING_GNN embeddings (256-dim) cannot adapt to task-specific perturbation patterns, limiting fusion effectiveness
- Head overcapacity relative to trainable backbone parameters contributes to overfitting despite weight_decay=0.01

children.name: node3-3-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- AIDO.Cell LoRA rank increased: r=16 → r=32 (higher capacity)
- STRING_GNN partially unfrozen: mps.6+mps.7+post_mp trainable at lr=1e-5 (frozen prefix as precomputed buffer)
- Fusion simplified: gated projection → simple concatenation+projection ([640∥256]→Linear(896→640)→GELU→LayerNorm)
- Regularization strengthened: head dropout=0.3, 3-group weight decay (backbone 0.01, GNN 0.01, head 0.05)
- Class weights softened: [10.91, 1.0, 29.62] → sqrt-inverse-frequency [3.3, 1.0, 5.44]

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4683 vs 0.4797 (Δ -0.0114, regression)
- Val F1 peak: 0.4682 @ epoch 41 vs 0.4795 @ epoch 35 (Δ -0.0113)
- Val plateau: ~0.459±0.004 from epoch 41–81 (early stopping)
- Train loss: 0.025 (vs parent 0.044, better fit)
- Val loss: 0.841 (vs parent 3.004 at epoch 75, much better calibration)
- Train/val divergence ratio: 33.6× vs 68× (2× improvement, overfitting reduced)

**Key Issues**
- **Over-softened class weights**: up-regulated weight reduced from 29.62× to 5.44× (5.4-fold reduction)
- Insufficient gradient signal for rare up-regulated class (3% of labels), critical for per-gene macro-F1
- Parent's aggressive class weights were essential for minority class discrimination; calibration overfitting was symptom, not cause

children.name: node3-3-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Restored strong inverse-frequency class weights [10.91, 1.0, 29.62] (vs parent's sqrt-inverse [3.3, 1.0, 5.44])
- Replaced CosineAnnealingLR with cosine warm restarts (5-epoch warmup + T_0=30-epoch cycles via LambdaLR)
- Reduced decoder weight decay from 0.05 to 0.01 for AdamW consistency

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4753 (+0.0070 vs parent 0.4683, still −0.0044 vs grandparent node3-3 0.4797)
- Best val F1: 0.4751 at epoch 163 (total 229 epochs)
- Val F1 warm restart staircase: 0.454→0.460→0.466→0.469→0.470→0.475 across 7 cycles
- Train/val loss ratio: 116× at epoch 180 (train=0.032, val=3.77) — significantly worse than parent's 33.6×

**Key Issues**
- Severe calibration overfitting: extreme train/val loss divergence (116×) despite stable F1 due to strong class weights maintaining correct argmax predictions
- Warm restart cycle length (T_0=30 epochs) potentially too short for deep per-cycle convergence
- Calibration divergence limiting further F1 gains through poor probability output generalization

tree.name: node4
tree.memory:
**Technical Implementation**
- STRING_GNN transductive GNN backbone (5.43M params, full fine-tuning)
- Bilinear interaction head: projects perturbed gene's 256-dim PPI embedding to [B, 3, 256], dot-products with learnable output-gene embeddings [6640, 256] to produce logits [B, 3, 6640]
- Weighted cross-entropy loss (weights 12.28/1.12/33.33 for down/neutral/up classes)
- AdamW optimizer (lr=1e-4, wd=1e-4), ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler
- Early stopping with patience=20, trained on 2 GPUs

**Results & Metrics**
- Test F1: 0.4258 (best val F1=0.4260 at epoch 32, official test score=0.42575)
- Trained for 53 epochs total
- Training loss decreased monotonically: 0.968→0.402
- Validation loss increased from epoch 5: 0.706→0.888
- Train-val loss gap at best epoch: 0.382
- Class imbalance: 88.9% class-0 (neutral)
- Rapid F1 improvement phase (0.244→0.403) in epochs 0-10, then plateau (0.403→0.426) over 42 epochs

**Key Issues**
- Mild calibration overfitting: validation F1 stabilized while validation loss continuously increased
- Representational capacity ceiling of PPI-only topology
- After extracting coarse-grained PPI signal (epochs 0-10), backbone cannot provide discriminative information for fine-grained perturbation cascades
- Lacks transcriptomic or regulatory context beyond graph connectivity
- Argmax-level predictions stabilized early while probability calibration degraded

children.name: node4-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Per-sample cond_emb injection: 256-dim all-ones vector at perturbed gene propagated through 8 GCN layers
- Multi-layer attention-weighted fusion of all 9 hidden states
- 2-layer MLP interaction head (256→512→3×256) vs parent's bilinear head
- 10× stronger weight_decay: 1e-3 vs parent's 1e-4
- CosineAnnealingLR scheduler vs parent's ReduceLROnPlateau

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4024 vs parent 0.4258 (Δ -0.0234, regression)
- Val F1: 0.4027 at epoch 166 vs parent 0.4260 at epoch 32
- Training epochs: 191 vs parent 53
- Parameters: 7.65M vs parent 5.43M
- Train_loss: plateaued at ~0.89 vs parent 0.402
- Val_loss: increased from 1.137→1.190 vs parent 0.706→0.888
- Worst test outcome among all nodes so far

**Key Issues**
- Over-regularization from weight_decay=1e-3 prevents effective training data fitting (train_loss stalled at 0.89 across all 191 epochs)
- Cond_emb signal dilution: 256-dim all-ones vector at single node across 18,870-node graphs with 8 propagation layers produces severely diluted perturbation signal
- Simultaneous calibration overfitting (val_loss increasing) and underfitting (train_loss plateauing)

children.name: node4-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Adopted proven tree-best architecture from node2-1-3: partial STRING_GNN fine-tuning (frozen mps.0-5, trainable mps.6-7+post_mp, ~198K params) with 6-layer deep residual bilinear MLP head (rank=512, ~16.9M params)
- Reverted weight_decay from 1e-3 to 1e-4 ( abandoning parent's over-regularization strategy)
- Replaced parent's failed cond_emb injection and multi-layer attention fusion with focal cross-entropy loss (gamma=2.0, class weights [2.0, 0.5, 4.0])
- Used two-group AdamW: backbone_lr=5e-5, head_lr=5e-4

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4704 vs parent 0.4024 (+0.068 improvement)
- Grandparent node4 F1: 0.4258 (+0.0446 improvement)
- Tree-best gap: 0.034 below node2-1-3 (0.5047)
- Training duration: 150 epochs, best at epoch 139 (val F1=0.4704)
- Val loss at best: 0.3802 vs train loss 0.0404 (9.4× ratio indicating severe overfitting)

**Key Issues**
- Cyclic cosine LR schedule: total_steps=1200 vs actual ~6600 steps causes LambdaLR cosine to wrap past progress=1.0 and cycle back to full LR every ~25 epochs, generating oscillatory multi-wave learning phases instead of monotonic decay
- Calibration overfitting: 16.9M-parameter head severely overfits 1,416 training samples, with dropout=0.2 insufficient (val/train loss ratio of 9.4× at best epoch)
- Performance gap from node2-1-3 (0.034 F1) attributed to LR schedule mismatch and head overfitting rather than architecture differences

children.name: node4-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Fixed cosine LR schedule: total_steps corrected from 1200 to 6600 with progress clamping (eliminated cycling)
- Increased dropout: 0.2 → 0.3
- Increased head weight decay: 1e-4 → 1e-3
- Extended max_epochs: 150 → 200

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4770 (+0.0066 vs parent 0.4704, still −0.0277 vs tree-best 0.5047)
- Val/train loss ratio: 15.51× (vs parent 9.4×) — calibration significantly worsened
- Train loss: 0.0291, val_loss: 0.4512 at best epoch (severe divergence)
- LR schedule fix verified: val_f1 improved monotonically from epoch 40 to 173
- Early training instability: 41 regressions > 0.005 in epochs 0–40, −0.083 drop at epoch 6

**Key Issues**
- Insufficient warmup (200 steps) caused early training instability
- Structural overfitting: 16.9M parameter head vs 1,416 training samples (12,000:1 ratio)
- Stronger regularization (dropout 0.3, head_wd 1e-3) failed to prevent head memorization
- Val/train calibration gap dramatically increased despite increased regularization

children.name: node4-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Reduced head capacity: 6-layer rank=512 (~16.9M params) → 4-layer rank=256 (~4.5M params)
- Optimizer: AdamW → Muon (lr=0.005) for 2D weight matrices
- LR schedule: cosine with fixed schedule → cosine warm restarts (T_0=1200 steps, T_mult=2)
- Extended warmup: 200 steps → 500 steps
- Added label_smoothing=0.05
- Adjusted class weights to [2.0, 0.5, 4.0]

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4752 vs parent 0.4770 (−0.0018)
- Best val F1: 0.4752 at epoch 43; declined to 0.4623 by epoch 123
- Val/train loss ratio at best epoch: 2.72x vs parent 15.51x (improved)
- Early instability: 12 regressions vs parent 41 (improved)
- Val/train loss ratio by epoch 123: 4.79x (persistent overfitting)
- Tree-best gap: 0.0347 below node1-2-2-2-1 (0.5099)
- Second warm restart cycle peaked at val_f1=0.474, below first-cycle peak

**Key Issues**
- Warm restarts produced no staircase improvement; second cycle peaked below first cycle
- Backbone parameter LR-reset at each restart destabilizes embeddings
- Val/train loss ratio widened from 2.72x to 4.79x by final epoch
- Partial backbone fine-tuning with unstable GNN embedding gradient creates performance ceiling at ~0.475
- Head-to-sample ratio of 3,180:1 (4.5M params / 1,416 samples) remains too high despite reduction

children.name: node4-1-1-1-1-1
children.memory:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Fully frozen STRING_GNN backbone (5.4M params, all frozen vs. partial freeze with mps.6, mps.7, post_mp trainable)
- Reduced bilinear MLP head: rank=128 (vs. 256), 3 residual blocks (vs. 4), hidden_dim=512 with expand=4
- Reduced Muon LR: 0.003 (vs. 0.005) for 2D matrices
- Increased dropout: 0.45 (vs. implied lower value)
- Cosine warm restarts: warmup=500 steps, T_0=1200 steps, T_mult=2, patience=100

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test F1: 0.4728 (parent: 0.4752, regression of -0.0024; tree best: 0.5102, gap: -0.0374)
- Best validation F1: 0.4728 at epoch 78 (parent: 0.4752 at epoch 43)
- Val/train loss ratio at best: 2.43x (parent: 2.72x at best epoch, 4.79x at epoch 123)
- Training stopped: epoch 178 (parent: 123)
- Warm restart gain: +0.0020 (cycle 1 peak: 0.4708, cycle 2 peak: 0.4728 at step ~1700/epoch ~54)
- Training plateau std: 0.0018 over epochs 60-178
